name: Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail
name_original: Huéscar a Cerro Perico Ruiz — circular por el río Bravatas
description: Explore the moderate 11.6-mile Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail, a mountain biking route with 1442 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Huéscar offers diverse terrain within the Granada province of Spain. The region is situated at the foothills of the Sierra de La Sagra mountain range, which features peaks over 2,300 meters. Huéscar is also part of the UNESCO-recognized Granada Geopark, providing varied landscapes from fertile valleys to more arid areas and river systems. This environment presents a mix of challenging mountain trails, unpaved segments, and some paved surfaces suitable for different mountain biking disciplines. Best mountain bike trails around Huéscar The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñón del Toro and San Clemente Reservoir Loop, a 34.9 miles (56.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area around the San Clemente Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz, a difficult 46.7 miles (75.2 km) path. This route offers expansive views towards the Sierra de La Sagra. Local mountain bikers also love the Campo de Jurena Loop, a 16.8 miles (27.0 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Huéscar is defined by the Sierra de La Sagra mountains, the Granada Geopark, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Huéscar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 240 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Huéscar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Huéscar?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Huéscar, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 2 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Huéscar?
A: Huéscar offers highly varied terrain, from fertile valleys to rugged mountain ranges like the Sierra de La Sagra, and even arid areas within the Granada Geopark. You'll find a mix of mostly paved surfaces, unpaved segments, and technical sections including singletracks and ridgelines. The region is known for challenging trails with significant elevation changes, making it suitable for cross country (XC), trail, enduro, and downhill biking.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Huéscar?
A: The climate in Andalusia, where Huéscar is located, allows for mountain biking year-round. It's particularly popular as a winter sun riding destination, offering pleasant conditions when many other regions are too cold.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Huéscar?
A: Yes, Huéscar offers several circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199529164/huescar-to-mirador-de-la-sagra-circular-via-cerro-perico-ruiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 75.2 km path offering expansive views. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199721483/campo-de-jurena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Jurena Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27.0 km long and leads through varied rural landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Huéscar pass through diverse landscapes. You can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Clemente Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views of the Sierra de La Sagra, and experience the unique geology of the Granada Geopark. The region also features the Natural Area Secuoyas de la Losa with giant sequoias, and the Río Guardal with historical monuments like the Puente de las Ánimas.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Huéscar is known for its challenging trails, especially in the Sierra de La Sagra. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207380278/penon-del-toro-and-san-clemente-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Toro and San Clemente Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; (56.2 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199529164/huescar-to-mirador-de-la-sagra-circular-via-cerro-perico-ruiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz&lt;/a&gt; (75.2 km) are rated as difficult due to rugged terrain and significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199756537/great-trail-of-the-first-settlers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Trail of the First Settlers&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, stretching over 91 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Huéscar?
A: The mountain bike routes in Huéscar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Many riders praise the varied terrain, spectacular mountain views, and the opportunity to explore the unique landscapes of the Granada Geopark.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, there are several moderate trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199721483/campo-de-jurena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Jurena Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 27.0 km route that takes about 2 hours and 6 minutes to complete, leading through rural landscapes. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199698229/perico-ruiz-puente-roque-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.6 km long.

Q: Can I find places to stay or rest along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several shelters and huts that can serve as resting points. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3980308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo del Espino Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6002950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Cañada Humosa&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6373386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banderillas Shelter&lt;/a&gt;. These are often found in the more mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by mountain bike?
A: Many routes offer spectacular mountain views, especially those venturing into the Sierra de La Sagra. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199529164/huescar-to-mirador-de-la-sagra-circular-via-cerro-perico-ruiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is specifically designed to provide expansive views towards the Sierra de La Sagra mountain range.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in the Huéscar area besides mountain biking?

tips: It is a tough climb but the views of the surroundings are impressive. It is located a few kilometers from Huéscar.

faqs.question: Is the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail suitable for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: While komoot lists this route as a mountain biking trail, available research primarily identifies the "PR-A 304 Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque" as a hiking route located in the Geoparque de Granada, Andalusia, Spain. Specific details regarding its suitability for mountain biking, including terrain and dedicated mountain biking features, are not readily available. Mountain bikers should be prepared for conditions more typical of a hiking path.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail for mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as 'moderate' for mountain biking. However, given that it's primarily recognized as a hiking trail, specific mountain biking difficulty assessments are scarce. Riders should anticipate varied terrain that may not be optimized for bikes and could present challenges beyond a typical moderate mountain bike trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information directly associated with mountain biking access for this trail is not widely published. As it's known as a hiking route in the Geoparque de Granada, you would typically look for parking near common access points for the PR-A 304 route. It's advisable to check local maps or resources for the Geoparque de Granada for the most current parking options near the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route described as the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail on komoot is a circular route, meaning it forms a loop. This allows you to start and finish at the same point without needing to arrange a shuttle.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is primarily known as a hiking route, expect terrain that may include natural paths, possibly rocky or uneven sections, and dirt tracks. Specific details about its suitability or features for mountain biking are not available, so be prepared for a variety of surfaces that might not be purpose-built for bikes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can experience the natural beauty of the Geoparque de Granada. Notable points include the Cerro Perico Ruiz, which is a summit, and the nearby Huéscar Historic Center, a significant historical site.

faqs.question: Does the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other paths. You may find yourself on parts of the 'Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque' (56% of the route), 'Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada)' (41%), and 'El Camino Espiritual del Sur' (40%). Smaller portions also overlap with 'La atalaya del Muerto' and 'Sendero Puente Roque - Estación Tratamiento Agua Potable'.

faqs.question: Is the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a hiking route within the Geoparque de Granada, dogs are generally permitted, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail?
faqs.answer: The Geoparque de Granada region can experience hot summers. Therefore, spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November) are generally the most pleasant times to visit, offering milder temperatures and comfortable conditions for outdoor activities. Winter can also be suitable, though some higher elevations might experience colder weather.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque Trail?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the PR-A 304 Perico Ruiz - Puente Roque hiking trail within the Geoparque de Granada. However, it's always a good idea to check the official Geoparque de Granada website for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the town of Huéscar, where you can find various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. The Huéscar Historic Center is a highlight close to the route, making it a convenient place to find services before or after your ride.
